data.epi_content:
I first grabbed my pounding head, then tried to curse but despaired once more at the fact that no profanity would come out of my mouth.

Still, after brewing and drinking a cup of coffee and eating a few pieces of chocolate, the pain subsided somewhat.

At this point, coffee might as well be a universal remedy.

Come to think of it, I was supposed to attend class today.

And then Lydia would start bullying me.

Hmm.

I don't want to go.

Then I just won't go.

I'll kill time appropriately, and when I think I might get expelled, I'll start attending again.

With that thought, I throw myself onto the bed.

I could see the ceiling.

A familiar ceiling that evokes slight disgust every time I look at it.

I stretch my hand upward, but there's no sky to be seen, just the same white ceiling remaining.

The room is indeed peaceful.

Knock. Knock.

With a small knocking sound, someone entered my room without waiting for a response.

Seeing me with my hand stretched toward the ceiling, they tried to quietly close the door and leave, so I spoke first.

"Hey, you're not here to clean, are you?"

"Ah, yes! But I didn't know someone was here! I'm sorry!"

"It doesn't matter, come in."

I say this while lying on the bed, turning my head slightly.

After clinging to Evan yesterday and making a slightly embarrassing request, it's no wonder I'm feeling drained.

But, a guest.

Is she really a guest?

She's a servant who cleans my room.

I suppose she is, since she enters my room and chats with me about various things.

Let's treat her as a guest.

Since she's a commoner, even powerless me could easily silence her if needed.

...............

After welcoming this little maid who's been coming to my room for several days and chatting with her, I've become slightly troubled.

I wonder if I've made a friend, but thinking about it, rather than a friend, it feels more like I've gained something annoying.

"Miss, no matter how thoroughly I clean, if you mess up your room this badly, it takes too much magical energy..."

The nameless maid, who unfortunately has to clean what is probably the dirtiest room among all student rooms, complains but still diligently scrubs the floor, changes the bedsheets, and puts on new blankets.

Additionally, she cleans up the shattered glass pieces scattered on the floor and places new cups in the cup holder.

"That's your job, isn't it?"

With no one to meet and no desire to go anywhere, I just sit in a chair watching my dusty room.

"Dust probably generates naturally, and while others go outside to expel it all, I'm here alone sadly exhaling dust by myself."

"If you're going to talk nonsense like that, couldn't you go do it in a friend's room?"

"If I had friends, I wouldn't be pathetically sitting here while you clean."

"Oh, I'm sorry. I guess if you're here all the time, you really don't have anyone to talk to."

"Could you stop getting on my nerves?"

"Well, what does it matter? I don't think there's anyone else who would have a normal conversation with you, Miss."

"That's not true. There are maybe two people who might talk to me if I cling to them."

Evan and Vivian.

And the Crown Prince who told me to come find him?

Come to think of it, that's three people.

But I probably won't go begging the Crown Prince to save me, so he doesn't count.

The person who benefits most from my family's downfall is the one sitting at the top of the Empire.
data.epi_content2:
So I'd rather keep my distance from the person who will inherit that position.

"...Is that so?"

"Don't look at me with pity. You know too.

The reason there's no one around me, and the reason I don't leave here is because of my family, which has not only collapsed but is buried deep underground."

"Even so, is that how all noble ladies like you make friends?"

I was about to answer "of course," but the thought of Vivian crossed my mind, so I stopped.

By the time the maid had changed all the bedding, I handed her a freshly brewed cup of coffee. She looked at me quizzically.

"Drink it. It's my personal gesture of goodwill to those who enter my room."

"Getting a drink from a noble lady is a new experience for me."

"Well, I'm only half one now."

"Still, once you graduate from here, you'll live a comfortable life by my standards, unlike me who's just a maid."

If the phrase "by my standards" hadn't been attached before "comfortable life," I might have been slightly offended.

"...If I graduate, that is."

The conversation is starting to feel uncomfortable.

"Are you done cleaning?"

"No, just under the bed left. Hmm, there's something smelly stuck there."

It's probably blood from when my arm was cut and I lost consciousness.

But to gloss over it, I say:

"I must have spilled coffee under there."

"...I suppose so."

If I say it's coffee, then it's coffee.

Even though she's a maid at the Academy, this skinny girl is about my age and a commoner.

She seems to have gotten used to my blatant excuses, as she didn't dig deeper like she did at first.

I may have nothing left, but the aristocratic pride still follows me.

It's been long since it became a mockery.

At least, not to this maid.

She recites a magic spell and creates a wind that sounds like an electric razor passing under the bed.

All sorts of sticky things spin around and fly directly into the garbage bag.

These days, they don't even check the stains on the blankets before folding and collecting them, which is quite convenient.

"I've cleaned everything spotlessly today too!

By the way, aren't you curious about my name, Miss? We've been talking face to face like this for four days now."

"What would I do with your name?

If you're hoping I'll take you to my mansion and employ you, you'd better give up such expectations."

The girl's face turns gloomy.

"The mansion is probably gone by now."

"...Oh."

"Goodbye."

"...Um, Miss."

The maid begins to ask with a slightly darkened face.

Last time, it was someone who looked about two years older than this child.

That was quite tiring.

They probably already served another noble, as they came here with the attitude that my room could be cleaned carelessly.

Something must have happened, as they quickly disappeared somewhere.

"What is it?"

"Nothing, nothing at all!"

Nothing, yeah right.
data.epi_content3:
She's probably afraid of how I might react if she asks her question.

I just won't open the door.

She may have a master key, but if I put a chair against it, the door will never open.

Unless she breaks it down.

"Knife, where did I put that knife?"

On the clean blanket, I take out a small dagger I used to stab my wrist before, just to feel a bit of thrill.

At least, unlike the crazy act of pulling a trigger against my head, this comes with the certainty that I won't die, so it's much healthier.

I don't really care if the blade is dirty and causes an infection.

"If I die from a high fever, Evan, that oh-so-great Evan who hasn't visited me for three days. Right.

He'll use his precious healing magic.

If I break this ring.

But it's the first gift I received.

I don't want to break it.

I don't like being sick either.

Just a slight sting is good."

I've been mumbling to myself a lot lately.

"Hmm. Hmm. Hmm."

I hum a little tune to set the mood.

It was by Eddie... what was it? Higgins. Right. A performer I used to listen to often, I couldn't forget.

"Actually, breaking it might be beneficial since I could meet him again and get a new ring.

I'm not even sure if I like it.

Sometimes when I look at this lady, she often clings but then does things that aren't helpful at all."

It feels like I'm talking to myself as if having a conversation.

I'm doing it unconsciously, I suppose.

I hope I'm not doing this in front of people.

"Those impulsive parts are all because of you.

At least I chose to die slowly by taking pills in my room rather than dying in such a flashy way."

It wasn't a choice; it was because you were too stupid to choose the right poison and couldn't die in one go.

"Ah, not knowing proper poison, eating whatever I could find, and dying a lonely death with no one coming to find me?"

I sneer.

I answer with an embarrassed laugh.

"When you put it that way, I have nothing to say."

Of course you don't.

"But you know what? Why do we come back in the first place?"

Is that what neither of us knows?

"Even if you ask me, you should be the one who knows."

"I don't know. It might be some hereditary bloodline passed down, or a curse cast by some unknown great magician. Does the distinction even matter?"

"A curse. That's, heh, terrible."

I slightly cut my wrist.

Vertically, just enough to stimulate the skin without drawing much blood.

Perhaps I need this different kind of stimulation because I've experienced too many intense things lately.

At least when the blade touches the skin and makes a shallow wound, my mind goes slightly numb and my body feels peaceful.

What was it called?

Right, let's say I'm bloodletting.

Do they have that here?

Who cares.

They say non-suicidal self-harm behaviors should be stopped because they eventually lead to suicide.

But I've already died once and regressed, so it should be fine, right?

Rather, it's because I don't want to die that I'm always seeking and experiencing new things.

Thrilling and novel things.

People have always been like that.

"Life feels so fake that I'm just leaving marks here.

Whoever it may be, Erica or whatever.

Proof that I was here."
